definition: Construct a sample-sample similarity network within each omics view using normalized distances or kernels, then fuse the view-specific networks into one cross-sample similarity matrix. For SPECTRA, this audits whether an evaluation patient, cell, or biospecimen is close to training samples across complementary molecular modalities rather than only one feature table.
example_usage: python metric_to_similarity.py --input fused_snf_pairwise.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --metric-kind similarity --metric-col fused_similarity

citations.title: A metagene based similarity network fusion approach for multi-omics data integration identified novel subtypes in renal cell carcinoma

evidence.summary: The paper describes similarity-based multi-omics integration that transforms each data type into a sample similarity matrix and fuses networks.
The method constructs sample similarity networks with a kernel and integrates them through message passing.

citations.title: A metagene based similarity network fusion approach for multi-omics data integration identified novel subtypes in renal cell carcinoma

evidence.summary: The authors state that metagene-based fusion improves patient similarity calculation and can extend to other multimodal datasets.

limitations: Fusion can hide which modality drives overlap unless view-specific curves are also reported.
Kernel bandwidths, feature filtering, missing-data handling, and batch correction can dominate the similarity matrix.
SNF-style similarities are transductive if fitted on train and evaluation samples together; report that choice explicitly.
quality_gates: Report each omics view, preprocessing, kernel, neighbor count, and fusion method.
Run per-view SPECTRA curves before or alongside the fused curve.
Check whether technical batch or cohort labels explain fused similarity.
